name: values_agree_lo_equals_hi

proof fn phi_1_values_agree_lo_equals_hi(som: StrictlyOrderedMap<SHTKey>, i: usize, v: ID)
    requires
        som.valid(),
        0 <= i < som.keys@.len(),
        som.vals@[i as int]@ == v@,
    ensures
        forall |j: int| #![auto] i <= j <= i ==> som.vals@[j]@ == v@,
{
}
reason: When lo == hi, `values_agree` checks a single element — the forall quantifier `lo <= i <= hi` degenerates to checking one index, which could mask off-by-one errors in callers that expect a range was validated

name: values_agree_false_branch_inconsistent
code:
proof fn phi_2_values_agree_false_branch_inconsistent(som: StrictlyOrderedMap<SHTKey>, lo: usize, hi: usize, v: ID)
    requires
        som.valid(),
        0 <= lo <= hi,
        hi < som.keys@.len(),
        som.vals@[hi as int]@ != v@,
        forall |i: int| #![auto] lo <= i < hi ==> som.vals@[i]@ == v@,
    ensures
        !(forall |i: int| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> som.vals@[i]@ == v@),
{
}
reason: The second return value `ret.1` represents "almost agrees" (all match except the last) — but when `ret.0` is false and `ret.1` is true, the hi element disagrees while all prior agree; when `ret.1` is false, some element before hi also disagrees, but the spec doesn't tell you which one

name: do_end_points_match_external_body_unsound
code:
proof fn phi_3_do_end_points_match_external_body_unsound(e1: EndPoint, e2: EndPoint)
    requires
        e1.id@ == e2.id@,
    ensures
        e1@ == e2@,
{
}
reason: `do_end_points_match` is external_body and trusts that byte-level comparison matches spec-level `AbstractEndPoint` equality — if the implementation uses a different comparison (e.g., pointer equality or partial comparison), the ensures would be unsound

name: map_valid_ghost_desync_possible
code:
proof fn phi_4_map_valid_ghost_desync_possible(som: StrictlyOrderedMap<SHTKey>, k: SHTKey)
    requires
        som.valid(),
        som.keys@.contains(k),
    ensures
        som.m@.contains_key(k),
{
}
reason: `map_valid` asserts `m@.dom() == keys@.to_set()` — but `m` is a Ghost field with no runtime enforcement, so the ghost map domain could theoretically diverge from the actual keys if any external_body function mutates keys without updating the ghost

name: values_agree_ret1_true_when_ret0_true
code:
proof fn phi_5_values_agree_ret1_true_when_ret0_true(som: StrictlyOrderedMap<SHTKey>, lo: usize, hi: usize, v: ID, ret: (bool, bool))
    requires
        som.valid(),
        0 <= lo <= hi,
        hi < som.keys@.len(),
        ret.0 == (forall |i: int| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> som.vals@[i]@ == v@),
        ret.0,
    ensures
        !ret.0 || ret.1 || !ret.1,
{
}
reason: When `ret.0` is true, the spec says nothing about `ret.1` — the second return value is only specified under `!ret.0`, leaving `ret.1` unconstrained when all values agree, which could cause callers to read garbage from the second field on the success path
